column9: Remembered as a mighty hunter who slew beasts such as the Scorpion and the Boar, immortalized as a constellation in the night sky
Known for his excessive self-love, leading to his demise as he fell in love with his own reflection in a pool and wasted away
Famed for his twelve labors, including slaying the Nemean Lion, capturing the Golden Hind of Artemis, and cleaning the Augean Stables
Renowned for beheading the Gorgon Medusa and using her severed head to rescue Andromeda from a sea monster
Celebrated for his invulnerability except for his heel, which became his downfall during the Trojan War, where he was struck by a poisoned arrow
Known for slaying the Minotaur in the Labyrinth of Crete and founding democracy in Athens, establishing himself as a hero and a wise king
Her abduction by Paris of Troy led to the Trojan War, symbolizing the devastating consequences of beauty and desire
Remembered for his loyalty and brotherly love, as depicted in his adventures with his twin brother Pollux, the Dioscuri
Known for his unwavering loyalty and brotherly bond with Castor, symbolizing the power of familial love and unity
Noted for his unclean stables, which Heracles cleaned in one day as one of his twelve labors, showcasing his strength and resourcefulness
Celebrated as a skilled huntress who participated in the Calydonian Boar Hunt and defeated her suitors in a footrace, symbolizing female prowess and independence
Renowned for his wisdom and just rule as king of Crete, as well as his judgment in the underworld as one of the three judges
Recognized as the father of Achilles and a hero in his own right, known for his participation in the Argonautic Expedition and his marriage to Thetis
Revered as a mighty warrior in the Trojan War, particularly for his role in the defense of the Greek ships and his tragic suicide after being denied Achilles' armor
Revered as the legendary founder of Rome and a hero of the Trojan War, as depicted in Virgil's Aeneid, where he flees Troy and embarks on a perilous journey
Remembered as the tragic king of Troy during the Trojan War, whose city fell due to the Greek stratagem of the Trojan Horse, leading to his death and the fall of Troy
Famed for his musical talents and his journey to the underworld to rescue his wife Eurydice, ultimately failing to bring her back due to his own lack of faith
Known for unwittingly fulfilling a prophecy by killing his father and marrying his mother, bringing about his own downfall and tragic fate as depicted in Greek tragedy
Celebrated for his cunning and resourcefulness, particularly in his long journey home after the Trojan War as recounted in Homer's Odyssey
Remembered for his wish for the golden touch, which turned everything he touched into gold, leading to his realization of the folly of his greed
Leader of the Greek forces in the Trojan War, known for his role in the sacrifice of his daughter Iphigenia and his tragic fate upon returning home
Known for opening a jar (often mistranslated as a box) containing all the evils of humanity, symbolizing the curiosity and folly of humans
Leader of the Argonauts in their quest for the Golden Fleece, known for his cunning and bravery in facing numerous challenges
Legendary founder of Thebes and introducer of the Greek alphabet, symbolizing the spread of civilization and culture
Famed for his craftsmanship and ingenuity, known for constructing the Labyrinth for King Minos and creating wings for himself and his son Icarus to escape Crete
Remembered for his attempt to escape Crete using wings made of feathers and wax, flying too close to the sun and plummeting into the sea when the wax melted
Infamous for her betrayal of her family and homeland for Jason's sake, committing atrocious acts of revenge, including the murder of her own children

column10: Medusa possesses the ability to turn anyone who gazes upon her into stone with her petrifying gaze
The Chimera can breathe fire from its lion's mouth and possesses the agility and ferocity of a lion combined with the cunning of a serpent
Endowed with immense strength and ferocity, the Minotaur is a formidable combatant, often depicted as dwelling in the labyrinth of Crete
Pegasus possesses the ability to fly with incredible speed and agility, making it a prized mount for heroes and gods alike
Cerberus is fiercely loyal and possesses immense strength, capable of deterring even the bravest of intruders with its ferocious demeanor
The Sphinx is known for posing riddles to travelers, devouring those who fail to answer correctly, and possesses the strength of a lion combined with the cunning of a human intellect
Echidna is known as the "Mother of Monsters," giving birth to numerous legendary creatures, and possesses a cunning intellect and serpentine agility
Geryon is a skilled herdsman, known for his ownership of a herd of red cattle coveted by Hercules as one of his labors
Scylla is a terror of the seas, capable of snatching sailors from passing ships with her multiple heads and devouring them
Charybdis creates powerful whirlpools capable of engulfing ships and dragging them beneath the sea, posing a deadly threat to sailors
Polyphemus possesses immense strength and ferocity, often depicted as wielding large boulders or tree trunks as weapons
Typhon is one of the most powerful adversaries in Greek mythology, capable of unleashing devastating storms, earthquakes, and volcanic eruptions
Enceladus possesses immense strength and resilience, capable of withstanding even the most powerful assaults from the gods
Polybotes possesses the strength of a titan, capable of causing earthquakes and tidal waves with his mighty blows
Alcyoneus possesses invincibility as long as he remains in his homeland, Thessaly, requiring a clever strategy to defeat him
Otus possesses immense strength and ferocity, capable of challenging the gods themselves in battle
Ephialtes possesses the strength and resilience of a titan, posing a significant threat to the divine order
Hippolytus possesses exceptional skill in hunting and equestrianism, as well as unwavering loyalty to his principles
Eurytus possesses unparalleled skill in archery, capable of hitting targets with pinpoint accuracy even at great distances
Pallas possesses exceptional skill in combat, wielding his weapons with precision and valor on the battlefield
Gration possesses strategic acumen and martial prowess, leading his forces with courage and determination in battle
Cacus possesses immense strength and cunning, known for his ability to steal cattle and elude capture using deceit and brute force
Mimas possesses incredible strength and resilience, capable of withstanding even the most powerful assaults from the gods
Porphyrion possesses immense strength and strategic cunning, leading the giants in their legendary battle against the Olympian gods
